location.office_time_required: four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices

level_signals: 6–8 years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, finance, or similar generalist roles
Owns and orchestrates Company Planning at executive level

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6–8 years of experience in strategy, operations, consulting, finance, or similar generalist roles; prior experience in a similar role within tech is a plus but not required.
Strong business acumen and an analytical mindset — you understand how different parts of a business connect, and you use data and logic to drive decisions.
Sharp problem-solving skills with a bias toward action and continuous improvement.
Strong communication and storytelling ability — you can distill complex inputs into clear, actionable narratives.
Comfortable working across teams, driving alignment, and influencing without authority.
Highly organized, with a knack for building structure in ambiguous situations.
A systems thinker who cares about how an organization operates and how to make it better.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: prior experience in a similar role within tech is a plus but not required.

scope.surface: Company Planning and operating cadences for Chime at the executive level; sits within COO org
primary_metrics_inferred: Quality and timeliness of company planning cycles
core_responsibilities: Run and improve Chime's operating cadences including annual planning, goal setting, business reviews, and product roadmapping
Drive cross-functional alignment across Product, Engineering, Marketing, Finance, People, and Communications
Enable executive decision-making by preparing materials and recommendations for leadership meetings
Optimize how Chime plans and operates by spotting improvement opportunities and leading projects
Support strategic initiatives and high-priority projects

green_flags: Role has clear ownership — company planning and operating cadences
Sits at the executive level within COO org, offering high visibility
Generous benefits including 24 weeks paid parental leave and substantial wellness/family support
Hybrid policy clearly articulated (4 days in office, Fridays remote)
red_flags: Four days per week in-office requirement is quite high and may not suit candidates seeking flexible hybrid arrangements
No visa sponsorship explicitly stated — unclear for international candidates
Years of experience range (6–8) framed as thriving criteria, but severity is ambiguous — treated as hard here

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years of experience, and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.cities_listed (no specific cities listed for this role; offices mentioned generally)
location.visa_sponsorship (not addressed in JD; set to unclear)
ambiguities: The JD hashtag #LI-Hybrid and the benefits section describe a hybrid model (4 days in office, Fridays remote), but also references a 'fully remote program' — it is unclear whether this role qualifies for the fully remote program or is strictly hybrid. Treated as hybrid with remote_us_eligible noted as true given the mention of a remote program.
The 6–8 years of experience is listed under 'To thrive in this role, you have' which could be interpreted as preferred rather than required. Treated as hard given the unconditional framing.
